MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Julie Hayashida of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant and moving party W.W. Grainger, Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Thomas Sylvester and Mary Ann Sylvester.

RULING: DEFENDANT W.W. GRAINGER INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant W.W. Grainger, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff Thomas Sylvester was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. (Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1442.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Sheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Defendant cannot rely on a plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Id. at 83; see Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 102-03.) Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. The Court did not consider Defendant's additional declaration of Emily D. Bergstrom in support of Defendant's motion and exhibits as it constitutes new evidence. (Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 253.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Janice H. MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than November 20, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than November 20, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 2, 2019 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 11, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 25, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by February 15, 2019 at 12:00p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, February 21, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/GLW)
